purpose: Resolve, normalize, and validate idempotency keys for mutation endpoints so duplicate submissions can be safely detected and replayed without duplicated side effects.

endpoints.description: Normalize an incoming idempotency key and return a stable lookup fingerprint.

sidecar_specification.purpose: Normalize and validate idempotency keys for mutation endpoints.

agent_usage.when_to_use: Use before processing non-idempotent writes such as payments, queue submissions, or upload issuance.
agent_usage.when_not_to_use: Do not use as the persistence store for replay records or as a cache eviction service.
agent_usage.example_workflow: 1) Receive a mutation request. 2) Call /idempotency/resolve. 3) Use the resolved key and fingerprint to check or persist a replay record.

failure_modes.condition: Key is missing, malformed, or inconsistent with a replayed request body
failure_modes.symptom: Rejected resolution with a clear reason
failure_modes.recovery: Provide a valid stable key and ensure retries use the same payload shape

forbidden_behaviors.description: Must not write outside declared scope
Must not make external network calls

agent_notes.implementation_hint: Trim and validate the supplied key, derive a request fingerprint from method, path, and canonicalized body shape, and reject mismatched replays early.
agent_notes.known_gotchas: Idempotency breaks when fingerprinting depends on unstable field order or timestamps, so canonicalize payloads before hashing.
agent_notes.preferred_approach: Return a normalized key plus a deterministic fingerprint that a separate replay store can persist.
agent_notes.avoid_approach: Do not store replay records or execute the underlying mutation inside this resolver.

test_cases.name: trims surrounding whitespace from idempotency key
